title.zh: 美元霸權的黃昏：川普時代如何動搖全球金融秩序
title.en: The Twilight of Dollar Dominance: How the Trump Era Shook the Foundations of Global Finance

summary.zh:
國際頂尖期刊《國際組織》最新研究指出，川普第二任期的政策正加速美元霸權的衰落。這不僅是單一貨幣的危機，更象徵著美國主導的戰後金融體系出現結構性裂痕。

研究團隊分析美元三大支柱——貿易結算、儲備貨幣與投資媒介——發現前所未有的同步弱化現象。與2008年金融危機後局部『去美元化』不同，當前危機源於全球對美國政治體制的信任崩壞：財政赤字惡化、行政機構遭政治化、經濟政策反覆無常，使各國被迫重構金融防線。

特別在金融制裁武器化後，中俄等國積極建立替代支付系統，歐盟推動歐元國際化，甚至傳統盟友也開始分散外匯儲備。但研究警告，網絡效應使美元仍具黏性，新興強權卻無意承接『金融公共品』供給者角色，世界可能步入既無單極霸權、又缺乏有效協調機制的『金融空窗期』。
summary.en:
A groundbreaking study in *International Organization* reveals how the second Trump administration has accelerated the erosion of US dollar hegemony. This isn't merely a currency story—it's a tectonic shift in the post-war financial order underpinned by American dominance.

The research identifies simultaneous weakening across all three pillars of dollar supremacy: trade invoicing, reserve currency status, and investment vehicle. Unlike the partial de-dollarization after the 2008 crisis, the current predicament stems from collapsing global confidence in US institutional stability—ballooning deficits, politicized governance, and erratic policies are forcing nations to hedge their financial exposures.

Notably, the weaponization of financial sanctions has spurred China's cross-border payment systems, Russia's gold-backed alternatives, and even EU efforts to boost the euro. Yet the study cautions that while network effects preserve dollar inertia, rising powers show little appetite to assume the burdens of financial leadership. We're entering a precarious 'interregnum'—a fragmented landscape where no single currency can replicate the dollar's historical role, nor can multilateral mechanisms effectively coordinate the transition.

keyArticle.title: Dollar Diminished: The Unmaking of US Financial Hegemony Under Trump
